project_level: Luke Thompson | Steven Auscavitch | Nicole Miller | Clement Coclet
NOAA Ocean Exploration | NOAA National Systematics Laboratory | National Museum of Natural History | NOAA Atlantic Oceanographic and Meteorological Laboratory | Northern Gulf Institute
NOAA Ocean Exploration seawater eDNA metabarcoding

Sampling bags were new, sterile WhirPaks. Single-use syringes and filters were used for filtration. Single-use gloves were used to collect and filter each sample. All tools sterilized with 10% bleach and 70% ethanol between handling filters.

Eukarya-18S-V4-Stoeck-Piredda: Triplicate PCR reactions were run in 10 uL reaction volumes, with 1.0 uL of environmental DNA template, 5.0 uL of 2X GoTaq Hot Start, 3.3 uL of sterile water, 0.1 uL of Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA) (0.2 mg/mL), and 0.3 uL of each primer (10 uM).
Eukarya-18S-V9-Lane-Medlin: PCR reactions were run in 13.25 uL reaction volumes, with 2.0 uL of DNA, 6.25 uL of Kapa HiFi HotStart ReadyMix, and 2.5 uL of each primer (1 uM).
Metazoa-COI-Leray-Geller: Triplicate PCR reactions were run in 10 uL reaction volumes, with 1.0 uL of environmental DNA template, 5.0 uL of 2X GoTaq Hot Start, 3.3 uL of sterile water, 0.1 uL of Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA) (0.2 mg/mL), and 0.3 uL of each primer (10 uM).

Eukarya-18S-V9-Lane-Medlin: PCR reactions were run in 50 uL reaction volumes, with 20 uL of DNA PCR product, 25 uL of 2X Kapa HiFi HotStart ReadyMix, and 5 uL of Illumina index set.

project_level: Final library concentrations were quantified using QuBit or Quant-IT fluorometric assays. Library sizes were spot checked on an Agilent Tapestation High Sensitivity D1000 screentape.
